title: Smoky Irish Lamb Stew
description: A hearty, pillowy Irish stew featuring the rich flavors of lamb, sweet smoked paprika, and a tangy beurre-monte.
instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the lamb and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Remove the lamb from the pot and set aside.
Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped carrot and potato to the pot and cook for 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic to the pot and cook for 1 minute.
Sprinkle the flour over the vegetables and cook for 1 minute.
Add the smoked paprika, red wine, beef broth, salt, and black pepper to the pot.
Return the lamb to the pot and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, until the lamb is tender, about 2 hours.
Remove the lamb from the pot and shred into bite-sized pieces.
Strain the cooking liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean pot.
Add the beurre-monte to the pot and stir to combine.
Return the shredded lamb to the pot and stir to combine.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley if desired.
